implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002: Parameterised seven-stage retry-from-scratch with idempotent stage_log
AC-003: Mid-autobuild crash + authoritative durable history
AC-004: Notification publish failure does not block stage_log
AC-005: LTM seeder failure does not block stage_log
AC-006: FakeClock used throughout; no wall-clock waits
AC-007: Black-clean (88 chars, py311 target); py_compile OK

completion_promises.evidence: Created tests/integration/test_mode_a_crash_recovery.py containing 12 passing tests covering Group D crash-recovery, ASSUM-004 durable-vs-advisory, Group G publish-failure, Group I LTM-failure, and the FakeClock canary. Verified by `pytest tests/integration/test_mode_a_crash_recovery.py -v` → 12 passed.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Parameterised test covers all seven stage classes from the Scenario Outline; for each: drive supervisor to mid-flight; trigger fake crash; assert build re-enters preparing state; assert prior in-flight stage is reattempted from start; assert no duplicate stage_log entry written for the re-attempted stage (idempotent retry)
completion_promises.evidence: TestCrashRecoveryAcrossSevenStages.test_retry_from_scratch_after_crash is parameterised over SEVEN_STAGES = [PRODUCT_OWNER, ARCHITECT, SYSTEM_ARCH, SYSTEM_DESIGN, FEATURE_SPEC, FEATURE_PLAN, AUTOBUILD]. For each row: (1) seeds prereqs through the target stage via _seed_prereqs_through; (2) pre-crash supervisor.next_turn dispatches the target stage (asserted DISPATCHED + chosen_stage matches); (3) crash simulated by `del sup_pre` and `state_machine.reset_to_preparing(build_id)` then asserted `BuildState.PREPARING`; (4) fresh supervisor against the same DurableStageLog + AdvisoryAsyncTaskChannel re-dispatches the same stage; (5) `durable_log.approved_count(...) == 0` proves no duplicate stage_log approval was written. Two per-turn rows recorded (pre+post), zero approval rows. All 7 parameterised rows pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Mid-autobuild crash test: driver dispatches autobuild, waits until AutobuildState.lifecycle reaches running_wave; triggers crash; on restart, supervisor consults stage_log (authoritative) and ignores async_tasks channel data (advisory); asserts build restarts autobuild from scratch
completion_promises.evidence: TestMidAutobuildCrashAuthoritativeDurableHistory.test_crash_mid_running_wave_restarts_autobuild_from_scratch: (1) RecordingAutobuildDispatcher writes lifecycle='running_wave' to AdvisoryAsyncTaskChannel on dispatch (mirrors production); (2) test asserts running_wave is observed in advisory + stage_log shows AUTOBUILD NOT approved; (3) crash simulated; (4) fresh supervisor + fresh autobuild dispatcher; (5) post-crash dispatch occurs even though advisory still reports running_wave — proves durable history is authoritative; (6) post_dispatcher.calls confirms exactly one redispatch. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Notification-publish-failure test (Group G @data-integrity): stub publisher raises PublishFailure; assert stage still recorded as approved in stage_log; assert next stage's prerequisite still evaluates as satisfied
completion_promises.evidence: TestNotificationPublishFailure.test_publish_failure_does_not_roll_back_stage_log uses FailingPublisher (raises _PublishFailure as a local stand-in for forge.adapters.nats.pipeline_publisher.PublishFailure to keep this test scoped away from transport scaffolding) wired through StageLogFirstSpecialistDispatcher. After supervisor.next_turn: (1) publisher.calls == 1 and spec.publish_failed is True; (2) durable_log.is_approved(build_id, PRODUCT_OWNER) is True; (3) StageOrderingGuard().next_dispatchable returns ARCHITECT in the permitted set, proving ARCHITECT ← PRODUCT_OWNER prerequisite is still satisfied. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Long-term-memory-seeding-failure test (Group I @data-integrity): stub LTM seeder raises; assert stage still recorded as approved
completion_promises.evidence: TestLongTermMemorySeedingFailure.test_ltm_seeder_failure_does_not_roll_back_stage_log uses FailingLtmSeeder (raises _LtmSeederFailure) wired through StageLogFirstLtmAwareDispatcher. After supervisor.next_turn: (1) ltm_seeder.calls == 1 and spec.ltm_failed is True; (2) durable_log.is_approved(build_id, PRODUCT_OWNER) is True. Test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All tests use FakeClock; no real wall-clock waits
completion_promises.evidence: FakeClock class defined in the module with fixed UTC datetime callable. TestFakeClockCanary asserts (a) sequential calls return identical times (no wall-clock movement) and (b) caller-supplied fixed times are honoured. The parameterised seven-stage test exercises FakeClock() in each row. No test calls datetime.now(), time.sleep, or any other real-clock surface. The full suite runs in 0.03s, confirming zero wall-clock waits.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `python3 -m black --line-length 88 --target-version py311 tests/integration/test_mode_a_crash_recovery.py` and the file is now black-clean. `python3 -m py_compile` returns OK. The pyproject.toml does not enable any other linters in this project (no ruff/flake8/mypy declared). Re-running the suite after black formatting still shows 12 passed.
